name: 21-notebook-edit
description: Phase 6.9 backfill #160: exercises the NotebookEdit tool (Phase 4.3) — production `editNotebookTool()` at `src/tools/edit-notebook.ts:51`. The shared bilateral flow is intentionally minimal (1-turn no-tool, no harness tools registered) because the load-bearing surface here is the OR-side production `edit_notebook` tool's per-write source-normalization-to-`string[]` semantics + the four operations (`replace_source` / `insert` / `delete` / `change_type`) + the two scripted validation-error paths (`replace_source` without `new_source`; `insert` without `new_cell_type`). The Claude Agent SDK's built-in `NotebookEdit` tool runs inside the subprocess CLI and the harness uses MCP-bridge tools instead — there is no MCP analogue of `edit_notebook` registered on the Anthropic side, so the four-op flow + normalization + validation contracts are structurally inaccessible from the agent SDK's user-facing event stream. Same posture as PR #190 (`persistSession: false`) / PR #191 (allowed/disallowed grammar) / PR #192 (Enhanced Bash): bilateral parity carries hook-firing-order; OR-only supplemental carries the load-bearing assertions. The supplemental driver in `scenarios.comparative.test.ts` constructs an OR-only inline run with the production `editNotebookTool({cwd: tmpDir})` and a tmpdir-scoped fixture `.ipynb`, scripts six `edit_notebook` tool-use turns + a closing text turn against the in-process emulator, and asserts: (a) the four valid operations each return `{ok: true, cells: <count>}` with the expected cell-count progression; (b) the two validation-error operations each return `{error: <exact-message>}` from the production validation arms at `src/tools/edit-notebook.ts:104-119`; (c) the final on-disk notebook has every cell's `source` field as `string[]` (proving `stringToSourceArray` at `src/tools/edit-notebook.ts:39-49` ran on every successful write); (d) the cell-shape invariants from the production code — markdown cells lose `outputs`/`execution_count` on a code→markdown `change_type`, and `insert` of a markdown cell creates one WITHOUT `outputs`/`execution_count` — hold on disk after the run completes. The fixture is inlined into the supplemental (written to a tmpdir via `node:fs/promises.writeFile`) rather than checked in under `fixtures/*.ipynb` (PR body ambiguity call #2) — it keeps the parity-test surface fully self-contained and avoids a per-test-run static-file copy step. The initial fixture deliberately has cell 0's `source` as a STRING (not `string[]`) so a `change_type` run on it without `stringToSourceArray` normalization would leave the source as a non-array; the final-state assertion catches that.
prompt: Confirm the notebook editing tool is available.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.

content.text: The notebook editing tool is available.

Inserting a markdown cell at the tail.
Converting cell 0 from code to markdown.
Deleting the original markdown intro cell.
Done — applied all four operations and observed both validation paths.
